LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED, ON DEFENDANT UMG RECORDING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EX. C IS DENIED. THEREFORE, THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT UNDER THE UNLAWFUL PRONG OF THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW BASED ON FAILURE TO ESCHEAT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Continued To Aug-14-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Umg Recording, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested, on DEFENDANT UMG RECORDING, INC.'S Demurrer To Complaint. The Request for Judicial Notice of Ex. C is denied. Therefore, the demurrer to the first and second causes of action is overruled. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The Complaint does not state facts sufficient under the unlawful prong of the Unfair Competition Law based on failure to escheat. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
